pairs.question: What was the original name and location of 79 Railway Squadron before it became part of the British Army's modern-day 79 Railway Squadron?
pairs.answer: The original name was the Longmoor Military Railway, and it was located at Longmoor Military Camp in Hampshire. The squadron operated steam locomotives at this time.

pairs.question: What type of locomotives did 79 Railway Squadron operate after moving to Mönchengladbach in West Germany?
pairs.answer: After moving to Mönchengladbach, 79 Railway Squadron operated diesel locomotives, replacing their previous steam-powered fleet.

pairs.question: When did 79 Railway Squadron receive a major modernization and what changes were made?
pairs.answer: A multimillion-pound makeover began in 1983. As part of this modernization, new diesel locomotives were introduced, and the operating yard at Mönchengladbach was upgraded.

pairs.question: What was the new role of 79 Railway Squadron after it was relocated to Marchwood in 1999?
pairs.answer: After relocating to Marchwood near Southampton in 1999, the squadron was renamed to 79 Port Enabling Squadron, placed under the command of 17 Port & Maritime Regiment RLC, and expanded to include a troop of Vehicle Support Specialist (VSS) personnel.

pairs.question: What happened to 79 Railway Squadron on 13 May 2012?
pairs.answer: The squadron was disbanded at Marchwood on 13 May 2012. The Vehicle Support Specialists were reassigned to other units within the regiment, and the Military Railways capability was eventually lost with the disbandment of the Territorial Army unit 275 Railway Troop in 2014.

pairs.question: Which military regiment was 79 Railway Squadron part of in its modern configuration?
pairs.answer: The modern-day 79 Railway Squadron was part of the 17 Port and Maritime Regiment, The Royal Logistic Corps, of the British Army.

pairs.question: What event ultimately led to the loss of the Military Railways capability in the British Army?
pairs.answer: The loss of the Military Railways capability in the British Army occurred with the disbandment of the Territorial Army unit 275 Railway Troop in 2014, following the 2012 disbandment of 79 Railway Squadron.
